Justin Donofrio   Follow
Oct 11  •  picked 3 hours ago  

I, Too

The Harlem Renaissance is known as the cultural movement surrounding the gradual shift in black identity and political empowerment. One of the pioneering figures of this time period being Langston Hughes who was was an instrumental voice in the cultural shift toward acceptance of African Americans in the United States (National Gallery of Art); born on February 1st, 1902 and died on the 22nd of May, 1967 (The Poetry Foundation). In commemoration of his 122nd birthday I have decided to do a poetic analysis of his famous piece ‘I, Too’.
If you don't know me you, you might not get this poem.  Just remember my name is John. © Jun 7   
Read more →

Lycaenida   Follow
Jan 3  •  picked 1 hour ago  

A Morning In Italy

Elders cigarette ashes now lie,
Empty coffee cups, warmth under the sky.
Birds greet the morn, winds softly blow,
Hair tousled by breezes that gently flow.

Sunlight streams into my room's embrace,
New plants bask but slightly misplaced.
A book within my hands takes command,
And all needless plans slip like sand.
© Jan 3   nature

Angel Eater   Follow
Feb '20  •  picked 1 day ago  

The Blue Rose

His eyes widen
My preposterous question lingers in my head
Contemplating came easy
But it had already slipped out of my mouth
"I like you"
A gust of wind carries a blue rose
Then falls on to a burgundy box
It was a present
A present in which he was unaware that he was giving me
He laughs
I don't know what I was expecting
But at the very least I got to see his smile one last time
Read more →

Latest facebook Follow us

Indian Summer by William Wilfred Campbell

Along the line of smoky hills
The crimson forest stands,
And all the day the blue-jay calls
Throughout the autumn lands.

Now by the brook the maple leans
With all his glory spread,
And all the sumachs on the hills
Have turned their green to red.

Now by great marshes wrapt in mist,
Or past some river's mouth,
Throughout the long, still autumn day
Wild birds are flying south.

--

Commentary and details available https://allpoetry.com/poem/8484509-Indian-Summer-by-William-Wilfred-Campbell
